stanzas.sanskrit:
सान्द्रानन्दावबोधात्मकमनुपमितं कालदेशावधिभ्यां
निर्मुक्तं नित्यमुक्तं निगमशतसहस्रेण निर्भास्यमानम् ।
अस्पष्टं दृष्टमात्रे पुनरुरुपुरुषार्थात्मकं ब्रह्म तत्वं
तत्तावद्भाति साक्षाद् गुरुपवनपुरे हन्त भाग्यं जनानाम् ॥ १ ॥

gloss_en.en: of the nature of intense bliss and supreme consciousness
free from limitations of time and space
not easy to grasp at first

stanzas.meaning_en: The Supreme Reality (Brahma-tattva)—dense, concentrated bliss and pure consciousness—free from time and space, ever free, and pointed to by innumerable scriptures though hard to grasp at first. Upon realization it is liberation itself. That very Reality shines directly in Guruvayur. What good fortune for people.
stanzas.commentary_en: The Brahma Tatwam is concentrated Bliss, incomparable, not time / space bound and cannot be explained by even thousand vedas and scriptures. It is not easy to understand but those who have attained it are liberated. That Supreme Bliss shines in concrete form as an image of Sri Krishna in the Guruvayoor Temple. Those who come to Guruvayoor and surrender at HIS feet with devotion get that eternal bliss easily. Mankind is so fortunate that way.

must_show: A clear frontal Guruvayurappan-inspired Krishna murti standing under an ornate golden arch within a temple sanctum; maroon and gold color scheme; traditional oil lamp in foreground; full peacock feather crown visible; hands and feet fully in frame with correct fingers/toes
A symbolic presence of Brahma-tattva: a vast radiant ocean/field of consciousness and bliss surrounding or behind, beyond time and space
Subtle symbols of time and space dissolving (e.g., a fading clock and compass/coordinates) without readable text
Hints of many scriptures/Upanishads as abstract palm-leaf manuscript shapes or glowing scroll silhouettes (no readable letters)
scene_brief.composition: 16:9 teaching illustration; temple sanctum foreground, cosmic Brahman backdrop; strong central focus; no readable text
scene_brief.tone: awe + reverence; fortunate devotees implied (small silhouettes with folded hands)

stanzas.translation_en: The Supreme Reality-The Brahma Tatwa ,which is dense concentrated Bliss, which is of the nature of the Pure Consciousness, which is without parallel or comparison, and is totally free of time and space limitations, and is always free (of Maya) which hundreds and thousands of the Upanishadic statements seek to explain, and yet, is not clear. This Brahma Tatwa which is not easy to grasp in the beginning, but the realisation of which, is the highest purusharth (i.e. liberation), that (very Reality) shines right in front (in concrete form), (as an Image of Shri Krishna) in the Guruvaayur temple. Oh (wonderful indeed) is the good fortune of the people (who seek Thy Grace).
stanzas.stanza_roman:
sāndrānandāvabōdhātmakamanupamitaṃ kāladēśāvadhibhyāṃ
nirmuktaṃ nityamuktaṃ nigamaśatasahasrēṇa nirbhāsyamānam ।
aspaṣṭaṃ dṛṣṭamātrē punarurupuruṣārthātmakaṃ brahma tatvaṃ
tattāvadbhāti sākṣād gurupavanapurē hanta bhāgyaṃ janānām ॥ 1 ॥

stanzas.sanskrit:
एवंदुर्लभ्यवस्तुन्यपि सुलभतया हस्तलब्धे यदन्यत्
तन्वा वाचा धिया वा भजति बत जनः क्षुद्रतैव स्फुटेयम् ।
एते तावद्वयं तु स्थिरतरमनसा विश्वपीडापहत्यै
निश्शेषात्मानमेनं गुरुपवनपुराधीशमेवाश्रयामः ॥ २ ॥
stanzas.meaning_en: Though the most rare Reality is so easily at hand, people still chase petty worldly pleasures with body, speech, and mind. We, however, with firm resolve, surrender to the Lord of Guruvayur—the Self of all—for the complete removal of worldly suffering.
stanzas.commentary_en: Such rare things are so easily accessible to all. But many get easily distracted to worldly pleasures. However, Bhagwan’s devotees surrender with fully resolved minds for the eradication of all worldly sufferings.

must_show: A radiant Guruvayur temple sanctum with the accessible Krishna (Guruvayurappan-inspired murti) present clearly

A symbolic staircase or path showing two directions: upward virtues (spiritual knowledge, sense control, austerity, study of Veda, acceptance of spiritual master) versus downward vices (lust, greed, pride, anger, envy, ignorance, false prestige, attachment)
A small group of calm devotees surrendering with folded hands choosing the upward path toward the sanctum
A contrasting group being pulled toward the lower vices
scene_brief.composition: 16:9 teaching illustration; clear labels are NOT allowed; depict virtues/vices purely through symbolism (icons/gestures), not readable text
scene_brief.tone: contrast restless vs serene; devotional reverence; no parody
avoid: any readable words in the image

stanzas.translation_en: In this manner, such rare things are so easily accessible to all. Alas, people, pursue other worldly things with their body speech and mind. This, is pitiable indeed. However, we Thy devotees, surrender , to Thee, O Lord of Guruvaayur who is the embodiment of the entire soul of all beings of the universe, with fully resolved minds for the eradication of all worldly sufferings.
stanzas.stanza_roman:
ēvandurlabhyavastunyapi sulabhatayā hastalabdhē yadanyat
tanvā vāchā dhiyā vā bhajati bata janaḥ kṣudrataiva sphuṭēyam ।
ētē tāvadvayaṃ tu sthirataramanasā viśvapīḍāpahatyai
niśśēṣātmānamēnaṃ gurupavanapurādhīśamēvāśrayāmaḥ ॥ 2 ॥

stanzas.sanskrit:
सत्त्वं यत्तत् पराभ्यामपरिकलनतो निर्मलं तेन तावत्
भूतैर्भूतेन्द्रियैस्ते वपुरिति बहुशः श्रूयते व्यासवाक्यम्।
तत् स्वच्छ्त्वाद्यदाच्छादितपरसुखचिद्गर्भनिर्भासरूपं
तस्मिन् धन्या रमन्ते श्रुतिमतिमधुरे सुग्रहे विग्रहे ते ॥ ३ ॥
stanzas.meaning_en: That pure sattva—unmixed with rajas and tamas—forms Your divine body (made of subtle elements and senses), as Vyasa repeatedly declares. Because of its clarity, it shines as the splendor of supreme bliss and consciousness within. The fortunate revel in that easily grasped form, sweet to the ears and mind.
stanzas.commentary_en: Bhagwan’s form is the essence of the pure Sattva guna. Sage Vyasa remarks that Bhagwan’s form reveals Supreme Bliss & Consciousness. HE is the embodiment of beauty to those who have done good deeds in their life.

image.alt: The pure sattva form revealing bliss and consciousness

must_show: A pure luminous Brahman ocean (still, infinite) with a gentle wave of pure sattva arising
From that wave, the divine form of Krishna appears as a resplendent sattva body (subtle elements implied), revealing bliss and consciousness
A small group of fortunate devotees beholding with delight (ears/eyes suggested symbolically)
scene_brief.composition: 16:9 teaching illustration; minimal and symbolic; focus on wave-to-form emergence; no readable text
scene_brief.tone: pure, serene, radiant; beauty and clarity

stanzas.translation_en: That Satva Guna, which compared to the other two Gunas (Rajas and Tamas) is absolutely pure and not tainted by them. From such pure Satva guna,Thy form was born, constituted of subtle elements and organs of senses and actions. It is thus stated by sage Vyaasa, again and again, in many scriptures.Thy form, because of such purity, totally reveals the Supreme Bliss and Consciousness within (which is the resplendent Brahman) without any obstruction. In that form, the fortunate ones (who have good deeds to their credit) revel. That form is delightful to the mind and ears, which the devotees can easily grasp in Thy image.
stanzas.stanza_roman:
sattvaṃ yattat parābhyāmaparikalanatō nirmalaṃ tēna tāvat
bhūtairbhūtēndriyaistē vapuriti bahuśaḥ śrūyatē vyāsavākyam।
tat svachchtvādyadāchChāditaparasukhachidgarbhanirbhāsarūpaṃ
tasmin dhanyā ramantē śrutimatimadhurē sugrahē vigrahē tē ॥ 3 ॥

stanzas.commentary_en: Bhagwan Krishna is the pure Brahman like the mighty ocean, changeless, ever full, and of the nature of unlimited nectarine bliss. Like many pearls in the ocean, innumerable liberated souls are absorbed in Bhagwan’s Brahmic bliss. Bhagwan is the Shuddha Satwa Nishkala Poorna avatara Swaroopam.

must_show: A clear symbolic teaching illustration matching the sloka meaning
scene_brief.composition: 16:9; simple; central focus; no readable text

stanzas.translation_en: Oh Infinite Lord! Thou are the pure Brahman like the mighty ocean, changeless, ever full, and of the nature of unlimited nectarine bliss. Like many pearls in the ocean, innumerable liberated souls are absorbed in Thy Brahmic bliss and hence it is extremely resplendent. Just like the waves rise in the ocean, it is said that Thy Shuddha Satwa form has risen from the Brahman. Since the term Ansha-avataar (Sakalaa) is meant only for Thy other part incarnations. Hence why cannot Thou be called as Poorna-avataar (Nishkalaa)?
stanzas.stanza_roman:
niṣkampē nityapūrṇē niravadhiparamānandapīyūṣarūpē
nirlīnānēkamuktāvalisubhagatamē nirmalabrahmasindhau ।
kallōlōllāsatulyaṃ khalu vimalataraṃ sattvamāhustadātmā
kasmānnō niṣkalastvaṃ sakala iti vachastvatkalāsvēva bhūman ॥ 4 ॥

stanzas.commentary_en: Even though Bhagwan is  not bound by any activities or motives, HE resorts to the activity of creation. Because of that only 'Prakriti' manifests itself. Prakriti, exists in Bhagwan but as if non existent in the beginning of a new cycle of creation. Then, from the pure Saatvik portion of Prakriti, Bhagwan manifests in full majestic glory.

must_show: A clear symbolic teaching illustration matching the sloka meaning
scene_brief.composition: 16:9; simple; central focus; no readable text

stanzas.translation_en: Oh Birthless Lord! Even though Thou are not bound by any activities, and Thou are without any motive,Thou do resort to that activity known as 'eekshanaa' - the will to create. Because of that only 'Prakriti' manifests itself. Prakriti, which is latent in Thee, as if non existent in the beginning of a new cycle of creation. Then, from the pure Saatvik portion of 'Prakriti' (Maya) which does not in any way obstruct Thy Brahmic effulgence, Oh Lord of Vaikunth, Thou manifest Thyself, in full glory, with a Divine form.
stanzas.stanza_roman:
nirvyāpārō'pi niṣkāraṇamaja bhajasē yatkriyāmīkṣaṇākhyāṃ
tēnaivōdēti līnā prakṛtirasatikalpā'pi kalpādikālē।
tasyāḥ saṃśuddhamaṃśaṃ kamapi tamatirōdhāyakaṃ sattvarūpaṃ
sa tvaṃ dhṛtvā dadhāsi svamahimavibhavākuṇṭha vaikuṇṭha rūpaṃ॥5॥

stanzas.commentary_en: Bhagwan form excels in the beauty of rain bearing clouds, a beautiful bunch of blue lotuses to the eyes of the people who have done meritorious deeds. Bhagwan resides  where Goddess Lakshmi delights. HE is the source of nectarine Bliss in the hearts of people who meditate.

must_show: A clear symbolic teaching illustration matching the sloka meaning
scene_brief.composition: 16:9; simple; central focus; no readable text

stanzas.translation_en: Thy form excels in the beauty of the fresh rain bearing clouds, and of a beautiful bunch of blue lotuses. Thy form is an embodiment of beauty, in the eyes of the people who have done meritorious deeds, and is a complete incarnation of all their good deeds. Thou are the abode where Goddess Laxmi delights without inhibition. Thou are the source of a torrential flow of nectarine Bliss, soaking the hearts of people who meditate. On that form of Thine I continuously meditate, O Lord of Guruvayur!
stanzas.stanza_roman:
tēnaivōdēti līnā prakṛtirasatikalpā'pi kalpādikālē।
tasyāḥ saṃśuddhamaṃśaṃ kamapi tamatirōdhāyakaṃ sattvarūpaṃ
sa tvaṃ dhṛtvā dadhāsi svamahimavibhavākuṇṭha vaikuṇṭha rūpaṃ॥5॥
tattē pratyagradhārādharalalitakalāyāvalīkēlikāraṃ
lāvaṇyasyaikasāraṃ sukṛtijanadṛśāṃ pūrṇapuṇyāvatāram।
lakṣmīniśśaṅkalīlānilayanamamṛtasyandasandōhamantaḥ
siñchat sañchintakānāṃ vapuranukalayē mārutāgāranātha ॥6॥

stanzas.commentary_en:
Bhagwan created universe and people ended up suffering caught in maaya. 
But if there were no creation, how could human beings enjoy the beauty and sweetness of Supreme Bliss Consciousness of Bhagwan & come out of the sufferings

must_show: A clear symbolic teaching illustration matching the sloka meaning
scene_brief.composition: 16:9; simple; central focus; no readable text

stanzas.translation_en: O Invincible Lord! Thy activity of creation is indeed a tragic sport, as it causes a lot of sufferings to the living beings. This is what I used to think, but now I do not think so. For if there were no creation, how could human beings enjoy the beauty and sweetness of Thy form which is so delightful to hear and ecstatic to behold, and thus revel in the ocean of Supreme-Bliss-Consciousness.
stanzas.stanza_roman:
lāvaṇyasyaikasāraṃ sukṛtijanadṛśāṃ pūrṇapuṇyāvatāram।
lakṣmīniśśaṅkalīlānilayanamamṛtasyandasandōhamantaḥ
siñchat sañchintakānāṃ vapuranukalayē mārutāgāranātha ॥6॥
kaṣṭā tē sṛṣṭichēṣṭā bahutarabhavakhēdāvahā jīvabhājā-
mityēvaṃ pūrvamālōchitamajita mayā naivamadyābhijānē।
nōchējjīvāḥ kathaṃ vā madhurataramidaṃ tvadvapuśchidrasārdraṃ
nētraiḥ śrōtraiścha pītvā paramarasasudhāmbhōdhipūrē ramēran॥7॥

stanzas.commentary_en: Bhagwan always blesses liberation to all the devotees who surrender to HIM. HE who is  accessible to every one and bestower of unlimited boons, Bhagwan is the unique Paarijaata tree . But, people with worldly desires, look for trivial blessings from the Kalpaka tree of the garden of Indra.

must_show: A clear symbolic teaching illustration matching the sloka meaning
scene_brief.composition: 16:9; simple; central focus; no readable text

stanzas.translation_en: O Lord Vishnu! for the devotees who surrender to Thee, Thou always confer, unasked for, not only wealth and other desires, but also liberation.Thus being accessible to every one and bestower of unlimited boons, Thou are the unique Paarijaata tree [parijaata: a heavenly flower]. Alas, the desire prompted hoards of people, in vain, long for trivial blessings from the Kalpaka tree of the garden of Indra. [Kalpaka: a wish fulfilling tree]
stanzas.stanza_roman:
mityēvaṃ pūrvamālōchitamajita mayā naivamadyābhijānē।
nōchējjīvāḥ kathaṃ vā madhurataramidaṃ tvadvapuśchidrasārdraṃ
nētraiḥ śrōtraiścha pītvā paramarasasudhāmbhōdhipūrē ramēran॥7॥
namrāṇāṃ sannidhattē satatamapi purastairanabhyarthitāna -
pyarthān kāmānajasraṃ vitarati paramānandasāndrāṃ gatiṃ cha।
itthaṃ niśśēṣalabhyō niravadhikaphalaḥ pārijātō harē tvaṃ
kṣudraṃ taṃ śakravāṭīdrumamabhilaṣati vyarthamarthivrajō'yam॥8॥

stanzas.commentary_en: All the other Gods,  out of compassion, fulfill the desires of their devotees. But Bhagwan’s compassion to HIS devotees offers liberation to them. While other Gods rule over the world with their powers, Bhagwan is the inner controller of all and rule over all beings and other gods as well as Bhagwan HIMSELF. Hence all the fortunate jeevas take immense delight in the Supreme Bliss of Bhagwan.

must_show: A clear symbolic teaching illustration matching the sloka meaning
scene_brief.composition: 16:9; simple; central focus; no readable text

stanzas.translation_en: O Lord Shri Krishna! while other gods out of compassion, fulfill the desires of their devotees, Thou out of Thy unique compassion, offer Thyself (liberation) to Thy devotees. While other gods rule over the world with the powers invested in them, Thou are the very inner controller of all and rule over all beings and other gods as well as Thyself. Hence all fortunate jeevas [souls] take immense delight in the inexhaustible bliss that Thou are. As for Thee, Thou are ever satisfied in Thyself and are the abode of incomparable attributes. O Lord! prostrations to Thee.
stanzas.stanza_roman:
pyarthān kāmānajasraṃ vitarati paramānandasāndrāṃ gatiṃ cha।
itthaṃ niśśēṣalabhyō niravadhikaphalaḥ pārijātō harē tvaṃ
kṣudraṃ taṃ śakravāṭīdrumamabhilaṣati vyarthamarthivrajō'yam॥8॥
kāruṇyātkāmamanyaṃ dadati khalu parē svātmadastvaṃ viśēṣā-
daiśvaryādīśatē'nyē jagati parajanē svātmanō'pīśvarastvam।
tvayyuchchairāramanti pratipadamadhurē chētanāḥ sphītabhāgyā-
stvaṃ chātmārāma ēvētyatulaguṇagaṇādhāra śaurē namastē॥9॥

stanzas.commentary_en:
Bhagwan’s lordliness consists in being the controller of all Gods from Lord Shankara onwards.Bhagwan’s prowess overcomes that of those who excel the whole world in prowess. Bhagwan’s pure fame is sung by even the most desireless sages. Lakshmi Devi always resides in Bhagwan’s bosom. Bhagwan is omniscient and there is no 
 trace of attachment.  Therefore, the term 'Bhagavan' is applicable to Lord Guruvayoorappan alone.

must_show: A clear symbolic teaching illustration matching the sloka meaning
scene_brief.composition: 16:9; simple; central focus; no readable text

stanzas.translation_en: O Lord of Guruvaayur! Thy lordliness consists in being the controller of all gods from Lord Shankara onwards.Thy prowess overcomes that of those who excel the whole world in prowess. Thy pure fame is sung by even the most desireless sages. Laxmi Devi always resides in Thy bosom. Thou are omniscient and there is not the slightest trace of attachment in Thee. Therefore, the term 'Bhagavan' is applicable to Thee alone.
stanzas.stanza_roman:
daiśvaryādīśatē'nyē jagati parajanē svātmanō'pīśvarastvam।
tvayyuchchairāramanti pratipadamadhurē chētanāḥ sphītabhāgyā-
stvaṃ chātmārāma ēvētyatulaguṇagaṇādhāra śaurē namastē॥9॥
aiśvaryaṃ śaṅkarādīśvaraviniyamanaṃ viśvatējōharāṇāṃ
tējassaṃhāri vīryaṃ vimalamapi yaśō nispṛhaiśchōpagītam।
aṅgāsaṅgā sadā śrīrakhilavidasi na kvāpi tē saṅgavārtā
tadvātāgāravāsin murahara bhagavachChabdamukhyāśrayō'si॥10॥
